title: Result of Testing Fixation for Overnight Staining of PBMCs
text:
Wash of Overnight Stained Samples
1 mL of PBS was added to all samples, which were then centrifuged at 300 xg for 5 minutes. Supernatent was discarded and samples were resuspended in 180 uL of PBS. The fixed and unfixed unstained samples were divided into 2 tubes each, of which one of the 2 would have 7-AAD added, and the other would serve as a true unstained sample for fixed and unfixed cells.
5 uL of 7-AAD viability dye was added to the fixed & unfixed overnight backbone stains, as well as the designated fixed & unfixed unstained samples. These were placed in the fridge to incubate for 5 minutes.


Compensation/Unmixing Issues
Fixed & Unfixed backbone stains were both unmixed with unfixed single color controls. After unmixing, there appeared to be some unmixing error especially for the CD4/CD8 markers. David & discussed several possible reasons for this. Since the PBMC samples were cryopresrved & thawed quickly, they could have been damaged in a way that impacted autofluoresence, and caused more than one AF signature. It is also possible that the fixed backbone stain needs to be unmixed with single color controls that have also been fixed, since fixation is known to somewhat alter the Af signature of cells.


Result of Fixed & Unfixed Backbone Stain on PBMCs
also include image of fixed & unfixed 7-AAD alone


Result of unfixed single color stains on PBMCs

title: CAR T Cell Full Panel & Backbone Attempt 2 Overnight Stain Results
text:
Note: Deviation from typical wash & Re-Stain of CAR Detection Reagent
Backbone and Full Panel stains run overnight were not incubated with 7-AAD viability dye before analysis.
After analysis, 2 uL of CAR detection reagent was added to the overnight full panel and backbone stains, which were then incubated for 15 minutes.
After incubation, these samples were washed with 1 mL of running buffer and centrifuged at 300 xg for 5 minutes, then resuspended in 180 uL of running buffer. 5 uL of 7-AAD was then added to both samples; they were incubated for 5 minutes before analysis.


Full Panel
The 30 min full panel stain had about 3% CAR positive population, however the overnight full panel had about 5% CAR positive, similar to the backbone sample. This similarity and the increase in CAR positive population could be due to non-specific staining of dead cells with CAR reagent.


Backbone
The 30 min backbone stain had about 11% CAR positive population, however the overnight backbone stain only had about 5% CAR positive, suggesting that using the CAR detection reagent before the rest of the antibodies is not effective for good resolution of CAR populations in overnight staining.


Full Panel With Rerun CAR Detection Reagent


Backbone With Rerun CAR Detection Reagent

title: CAR T Cell Full Panel and Backbone Stain Attempt 2
text: Result of Previous Day’s Overnight Stain

title: CAR T Cell Full Panel and Backbone Stain Attempt 2
text:
Full Panel
There was a clear population of CD3 positive lymphocytes present in this population, and a sufficient number of cells to collect over 100k events. However, there was not a large or particularly clear population positive for the CAR marker.
insert picture of scatter, CD3 and CAR marker plots here
It is possible that there inclusion of so many markers negatively impacted the staining and resolution of the CAR T detection reagent. There are several potential future experiments that could be run to assess this.
The next step planned is to stain CAR T cells with the CAR detection reagent overnight alongside the rest of the antibody cocktail, instead of staining seperately and washing off the CAR detection reagent before staining with the antibody cocktail.

title: CAR T Cell Full Panel and Backbone Stain Attempt 2
text:
Backbone
A smaller number of cells was available, with fewer CD3 positve cells compared to the sample stained with the full panel, however there was still a population of CD3 positive lymphocytes. Unlike the full panel, a resolvable population positive for the CAR detection marker was present. This supports the idea that the presense of so many antibodies in the full panel may be impacting the resolution of the CAR detection.

RR668 and Biotin-PE inclusion did not seem to negatively impact resolution of the backbone antibodies, as seen in comparison of populations in the full backbone stained sample, and the sample that did not include RR688 and PE. However, there were far fewer cells in the full backbone stain, so this is difficult to see.

In future experiments, it seems as though RR688 and Biotin-PE should be included in the backbone cocktail.

title: Results of 30 Minute Staining CAR T Attempt 1
text:
MACS Quant Analysis
Using the MACS Quant, it was evaluated that the sample stained for Attempt 1 did not actually contain an identifiable population of CAR T cells, therefore despite inclusion of the CAR T detection reagent, it was expected that there would not be a population positive for the CAR T marker.


Backbone Stain



Full Panel

title: Overnight Single Color Controls at Low Concentration
text:
In order to optimize the panel for overnight staining, it is nessecery to determine the appropriate antibody titrations to be used. Since the overnight stain involves much longer incubation time, it is likely that the original antibody quantities used will cause staining that is too bright or contribute to non-specific staining.
Single color controls could also be used to unmix the full cocktail sample that was incubated overnight at these lower concentrations.

title: Overnight Single Color Controls at Low Concentration
text:
Day 1

All antibodies that were used in the panel, as well as TruStain FcX blocking buffer were centrifuged at 4000 rpm for 1 minute
Enriched T cell sample was obtained and 150 uL per stain to be run (total of 26 colors, so about 4 mL) was aliquoted into a 5 mL FACS tube.

note it was later observed that the cells in the sample provided were not in good condition, and a large portion of them appeared to be dead. There were also visible white/grey chunks floating in the solution the cells were provided in.

20 uL of TruStain FcX was added to the FACS tube, sample was incubated in the fridge for a minimum of 5 minutes
After incubation, 150 uL of the cell sample was aliquoted into 26 wells of a 96-well plate, followed by addition of antibody. The sample and antibody were added at the locations and amounts dictated by the following:

The well plate was placed in the fridge to incubate overnight (19 hours)

title: Overnight Single Color Controls at Low Concentration
text:
Day 2

The well plate was removed from the fridge following incubation, and 100 uL of running buffer was added to each well that contained cells
The well plate was centrifuged at 300xg for 5 minutes to wash cells, supernatent was discarded and cells were resuspended in 180 uL of running buffer.
Samples from each well were aspirated to mix, then pipetted into 5 mL FACS tubes to be analyzed with the Aurora-4 spectral cytometer

title: Backbone Stain Protocol

title: Backbone Stain Protocol
text:
Day 1

Cryopreserved enriched T cells were thawed, centrifuged, and resuspended in a minimal amount of buffer solution
Fluorophore - conjugated antibodies, TruStain FcX (Fc blocking buffer), and SuperBright buffer (to mitigate aggregation of antibodies) were centrifuged at 4000 rpm for 1 minute
5 uL of TruStain FcX was added to the total enriched T cell sample volume, cell sample was incubated for at least 5 minutes in the fridge
The backbone cocktail (antibodies known to stain well for this panel) was prepared for 2 samples, according to the following table (the volumes of antibody listed are per sample). 2 uL of SuperBright was added to the eppendorf tube prior to antibodies to mitigate aggregation.




Marker
Fluorophore
Volume (uL)




CD62L
BUV395
5


CD4
BUV496
3


CD8
BUV510
2


CD45RO
BV570
5


CD3
Spark Blue 550
5


CD45RA
Alexa Fluor 700
1




25 uL of enriched T cell sample was aliquoted into 3 5 mL FACS tubes, tubes were labled UN (unstained), BB-30 minute (backbone stain, 30 minute incubation), and BB-ON (backbone stain, overnight)
20 uL of the backbone stain cocktail was added to the tubes labled BB-30 and BB-ON
BB-ON tube was placed in the fridge and left to incubate for 19 hours (overnight), and BB-30 was placed in the fridge and left to incubate for 30 minutes.
After 30 minutes, 1 mL of running buffer was added to BB-30 tube, and UN tube. Tubes were then centrifuged at 300xg for 5 minutes, supernatent was discarded
Pellets were resuspended in 180 uL of running buffer
5 uL of 7-AAD viability dye was added to both BB-30 and UN tubes (should not have been added to UN)
BB-30 and UN tubes were incubated in the fridge for 5 minutes
BB-30, UN, and full stain (prepared by Rediet and Rubi) were run using the Aurora-4 Spectral Cytometer

title: Backbone Stain Protocol
text:
Day 2

BB-ON tube was retrieved from fridge, 1 mL of running buffer was added and sample was centrifuged at 300xg for 5 minutes, supernatent was discarded
pellet was resuspended in 180 uL of running buffer
5 uL of 7-AAD viability dye was added to BB-ON tube
BB-ON tube was placed in fridge to incubate for 5 minutes
BB-ON, full stain-2 (at antibody concentrations previously tested, incubated overnight), and full stain-1 (at lower antibody concentrations, incubated overnight), were run using the Aurora-4 Spectral Cytometer

title: Protocol for CAR-T Cell 30 Minute & Overnight Staining
text:
Day 1

Cryopreserved CAR-T cells were thawed, centrifuged, and resuspended in a minimal amount of buffer solution. Some sample was aliquoted out to serve as unstained control (UN).
1 uL CAR-T detection was added to remaining cell solution. Cells were incubated for 15 minutes in the fridge, then washed with 1 mL of RB, centrifuged at 300 xg for 5 minutes, and resuspended in minimal RB.
Fluorophore - conjugated antibodies, TruStain FcX (Fc blocking buffer), and SuperBright buffer (to mitigate aggregation of antibodies) were centrifuged at 4000 rpm for 1 minute
5 uL of TruStain FcX was added to the total CAR-T cell sample volume, cell sample was incubated for at least 5 minutes in the fridge.
The backbone cocktail (antibodies known to stain well for this panel) was prepared for 2 samples, according to the following table (the volumes of antibody listed are per sample). 2 uL of SuperBright was added to the eppendorf tube prior to antibodies to mitigate aggregation.




Marker
Fluorophore
Volume (uL)




CD62L
BUV395
5


CD4
BUV496
3


CD8
BUV510
2


CD45RO
BV570
5


CD3
Spark Blue 550
5


CD45RA
Alexa Fluor 700
1


Biotin
PE
2


CD19
Real Red 668
5




CAR-T cell sample was aliquoted into 3 5 mL FACS tubes, tubes were labled UN (unstained), BB-30 minute (backbone stain, 30 minute incubation), and BB-ON (backbone stain, overnight).
27 uL of BB cocktail was add to the BB-30 minute and BB-ON samples
BB-30 minute was placed in fridge to incubate for 30 minutes, and BB-ON was placed in fridge to incubate overnight, UN was also placed in fridge.
After incubation, 1 mL of running buffer was added to BB-30 tube, and UN tube. Tubes were then centrifuged at 300xg for 5 minutes, supernatent was discarded.
Pellets were resuspended in 180 uL of running buffer
5 uL of 7-AAD viability dye was added to BB-30 tube and tube was placed in fridge to incubate for 5 minutes.
BB-30, UN, and full stain (prepared by Rediet & Rubi) were run using the Aurora-4 Spectral Cytometer

title: Protocol for CAR-T Cell 30 Minute & Overnight Staining
text:
Day 2

BB-ON tube was retrieved from fridge, 1 mL of running buffer was added and sample was centrifuged at 300xg for 5 minutes, supernatent was discarded
pellet was resuspended in 180 uL of running buffer
5 uL of 7-AAD viability dye was added to BB-ON tube
BB-ON tube was placed in fridge to incubate for 5 minutes
BB-ON and full stain incubated overnight were run using the Aurora-4 Spectral Cytometer

title: Results of Overnight CART Stain (Full and Backbone)
text: About 80% of cells appeared to be alive after overnight incubation in fridge, this was determined with 7-AAD viability dye. Markers included in the backbone stained well enough to allow for populations to be resolved, however, as anticipated based on analysis of the cells used with the MACSQuant, the CAR reagent & biotin markers (RR688 & PE fluorophores) did not show positive populations.

title: Results of Overnight CART Stain (Full and Backbone)
text:
Full Panel (No Medium)
Good marker resolution, however about 80% of cells were viable as determined with 7-AAD viability dye. No significant positive population was observed for CAR marker after gating on healthy, live, CD3 positive cells.

title: Results of Overnight CART Stain (Full and Backbone)
text:
Full Panel (Medium)
The inclusion of medium did increase the proportion of viable cells following overnight incubation as compared with the sample incubated in only running buffer. It appears that the inclusion of medium did impact antibody binding, and reduced resolution was observed for several markers when compared to the sample without medium.
will include images of the above here
It was determined that in future experiments, medium is likely not a useful way to account for cell death during the long incubation, due to the impact on antibody binding and population resolution. Possible next steps include the use of different medium, or fixing cells prior to staining.

title: Testing PFA for Overnight Staining of PBMCs
text:
Background
Dr. Fan, Rediet, and myself, have previously noticed and been concened by the proportion of cells that appear to die over the course of overnight incubation. Previously, one way that we have sought to increase the proportional of cells that remain viable is to incubate cells overnight in a complete medium, as opposed to PBS. This did improve the number of cells which survived overnight incubation, but had a detrimental effect on the resolution of many markers, and thus was deemed to be a poor solution.
Based on the protocols reccomended in the paper “Improving High Parameter Cytometry with Overnight Staining”, I decided to test fixation of the samples to be stained overnight.


Protocol
PBMCs that had been cyropreseved by David Rach were thawed, they were suspended in R10 (10% FBS, 1% L-glutamine, and 0.1% gentamycin) for cryopresevation. 2 mL of sample, containing 6 million samples, were retrieved. 650 uL of sample was aliquoted into 3 5-mL FACS tubes (2M cells per tube).
2 mL of PBS was added to each sample tube and samples were centrifuged at 300 xg for 5 minutes to wash away R10 solution. Supernatent was discarded and samples were resuspended in 100 uL of PBS. The sample that would be used for single-color stains was resuspended in 600 uL PBS (100 uL per antibody that would be part of the single stain).
5 uL of TruStain FcX was added to each sample, and samples were allowed to incubate in the fridge for at least 5 minutes.
While samples were incubating, 4% PFA fixative was obtained and diluted to 0.4% (10 uL of PFA and 90 uL of PBS were added to an eppendorf tube and mixed well).
After incubation with FcX, each tube (containing 100 uL of cell solution) was split into 2 50 uL aliquots. These 4 samples were labled “unstained, unstained-fixed, backbone, backbone-fixed”. 50 uL of the 0.4% PFA solution was added to the two samples labled for fixation, and these samples were incubated in the fridge for 20 minutes.
100 uL of the single-color sample was aliqoted into each of 6 tubes, labled with the antibody-fluorophore conjugate that would be added to that tube.
Backbone cocktail was prepared, containing the following:



Marker
Fluorophore
Volume (uL)




CD62L
BUV395
2


CD4
BUV496
1


CD8
BUV510
1


CD45RO
BV570
1


CD3
Spark Blue 550
1


CD45RA
Alexa Fluor 700
1



After fixation incubation, 500 uL of PBS was added to both samples containing fixative and these samples wee centrifuged at 300 xg for 5 minutes. Supernatent was discarded and samples were resuspended in 50 uL of PBS to match unfixed samples.
4.5 uL of the backbone cocktail was added to the unfixed and fixed backbone samples, and these samples were placed in the fridge to incubate overnight
Antibody stains were added to the 6 single color stain samples according to the table above, and single color stains were also placed in the fridge to incubate overnight.
